seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between St Johns Park and St Marys. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: St Johns Park vs St Marys: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in St Johns Park and St Marys.
heading: St Johns Park vs St Marys — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between St Johns Park, New South Wales and St Marys, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: St Marys has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to St Johns Park (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is St Johns Park or St Marys safer from flooding?
faq.answer: St Johns Park has a flood risk score of 29/100, while St Marys scores 27/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: St Johns Park scores 39/100 for bushfire risk. St Marys scores 42/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in St Johns Park or St Marys?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. St Marys has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.5% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
